title: Cut-Fill Balance Optimizer
description: Industrial-grade earthwork balancing tool for mass haul optimization, minimizing rehandle and haul costs using lean construction and Six Sigma principles.

inputs.label: Cut Volume
inputs.businessContext: Total volume of material to be excavated from cut areas, measured in bank cubic meters (BCM).

businessContext_i18n.en: Total volume of material to be excavated from cut areas, measured in bank cubic meters (BCM).

inputs.label: Fill Volume
inputs.businessContext: Total volume of material required for fill/embankment areas, measured in compacted cubic meters (CCM).

businessContext_i18n.en: Total volume of material required for fill/embankment areas, measured in compacted cubic meters (CCM).

inputs.label: Swell Factor
inputs.businessContext: Factor accounting for volume increase when material is excavated (loose state vs. bank state). Typical range 1.1–1.4.

businessContext_i18n.en: Factor accounting for volume increase when material is excavated (loose state vs. bank state). Typical range 1.1–1.4.

inputs.label: Shrinkage Factor
inputs.businessContext: Factor accounting for volume decrease when material is compacted (compacted state vs. bank state). Typical range 0.75–0.95.

businessContext_i18n.en: Factor accounting for volume decrease when material is compacted (compacted state vs. bank state). Typical range 0.75–0.95.

inputs.label: Average Haul Distance
inputs.businessContext: Average one-way distance from cut to fill areas, used for cost and fuel calculations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average one-way distance from cut to fill areas, used for cost and fuel calculations.

inputs.label: Unit Haul Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost per cubic meter per kilometer of hauling material, including fuel, labor, and equipment depreciation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost per cubic meter per kilometer of hauling material, including fuel, labor, and equipment depreciation.

inputs.label: Waste Disposal Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost per cubic meter for disposing excess material at a designated waste site.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost per cubic meter for disposing excess material at a designated waste site.

inputs.label: Borrow Material Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost per cubic meter to import material from a borrow pit to meet fill deficit.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost per cubic meter to import material from a borrow pit to meet fill deficit.

inputs.label: Material Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of material being moved, affects swell/shrinkage factors and handling difficulty.

businessContext_i18n.en: Type of material being moved, affects swell/shrinkage factors and handling difficulty.

inputs.label: Use Optimized Haul Routes
inputs.businessContext: If enabled, the tool applies lean logistics principles to minimize haul distance and idle time.

businessContext_i18n.en: If enabled, the tool applies lean logistics principles to minimize haul distance and idle time.

rules.message: Both cut and fill volumes must be greater than zero for a valid balance calculation.
Swell factor must be >= 1.0 and shrinkage factor <= 1.0 to reflect physical reality.
Unit haul cost must be positive.

thresholds.message: Cut volume exceeds fill volume by more than 30%. Consider waste disposal or design adjustment.
Fill volume exceeds cut volume by more than 30%. Consider borrow material or design adjustment.
Haul cost per cubic meter exceeds $5.00. Investigate alternative routes or equipment.
Cut-fill imbalance exceeds 50%. Major rehandle or import/export expected.

formulas.label: Adjusted Cut Volume (Bank to Compacted)
formulas.description: Converts cut volume from bank state to compacted state using shrinkage factor.

formulas.label: Adjusted Fill Volume (Bank to Loose for Haul)
formulas.description: Converts fill volume from compacted state to loose state using swell factor for haul cost calculation.

formulas.label: Net Balance (Compacted Basis)
formulas.description: Difference between adjusted cut and fill volumes on a compacted basis.

formulas.label: Excess Cut Volume (Waste)
formulas.description: Volume of cut material that cannot be used for fill, requiring disposal.

formulas.label: Deficit Fill Volume (Borrow)
formulas.description: Volume of fill material that must be imported from borrow pits.

formulas.label: Total Haul Volume (Loose Basis)
formulas.description: Total volume of material to be hauled, considering both cut-to-fill and borrow/waste movements.

formulas.label: Total Earthwork Cost
formulas.description: Primary result: total cost including haul, waste disposal, and borrow material.

primary.label: Total Earthwork Cost
primary.description: Aggregate cost of cut-fill balance operations including haul, waste, and borrow.

haulCost.label: Haul Cost
haulCost.description: Cost of transporting material from cut to fill and to/from waste/borrow sites.

wasteCost.label: Waste Disposal Cost
wasteCost.description: Cost of disposing excess cut material.

borrowCost.label: Borrow Material Cost
borrowCost.description: Cost of importing material to meet fill deficit.

netBalance.label: Net Balance (Compacted)
netBalance.description: Positive indicates excess cut, negative indicates deficit fill.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Rehandle Ratio
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Ratio of material moved more than once (e.g., stockpiled then hauled). High values indicate inefficiency.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Idle Equipment Time
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Estimated total idle time due to imbalance, based on standard cycle times.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Fuel Waste Factor
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Additional fuel consumption due to suboptimal haul routes (1.0 = optimal).

suggestedActions.label: Optimize Haul Routes
suggestedActions.description: Use GPS and site layout to reduce average haul distance by at least 10%.

suggestedActions.label: Revise Cut/Fill Design
suggestedActions.description: If net balance > 20% of total volume, consider regrading to reduce waste or borrow.

suggestedActions.label: On-Site Material Processing
suggestedActions.description: Crush rock or treat clay on-site to improve material usability and reduce borrow.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduces borrow cost and haul volume.

suggestedActions.label: Implement Lean Haul Practices
suggestedActions.description: Apply Six Sigma DMAIC to reduce cycle time variability and equipment idle time.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Improves productivity and reduces fuel waste.
